fields.description:
# Code-review impact model redesign: two-lane tier-tagged MAX with re-anchored scale

## Why
Production-severity axes + the mean structurally mis-measure code-review's maintainability/latent-regression
findings: a feature silently dead in prod (untested wiring) scores 0.415 because prod_impact is 'none' and
reversibility 'easy' drag it down. Landmines and nits overlap so no scalar threshold separates them.

## What
A code-review-specific, verifier-embedded impact model (`decide.impact_code`) dispatched per-gate off the
shared `decide.pass3_decide` via the `impact_fn` param (that param ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the
plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead; code-review now passes `impact_fn=impact_code`).
- TIER VALUES: each consequence binary carries the severity it implies if true — minor=0.3, moderate=0.6,
  serious=0.9. Lane-severity = MAX of the tiers of the TRUE binaries (no dilution; a minor/moderate binary
  cannot alone reach the block zone). No cross-binary compounding (conservative default).
- TWO LANES: `prod_lane = MAX(correctness tier-tagged binaries) * likelihood_mult`; `maint_lane =
  MAX(maintainability tier-tagged binaries) * freq_mult`. Each binary is assigned to EXACTLY one lane + one
  tier by an explicit table (below).
- LIKELIHOOD (LLM-emitted enum): `common=1.0 / sometimes=0.6 / rare=0.25`; absent => `common` (1.0) so a
  serious correctness binary is not silently dampened by missing metadata.
- CHANGE-FREQUENCY MULTIPLIER: `freq_mult = 0.5 + 0.5 * min(churn_90d, 30) / 30` where churn_90d = commits
  touching the finding's file in the last 90 days (`git log`, DET). Linear, saturates at 30. Applies to
  maint_lane only. FALLBACK: git unavailable / shallow clone / no file attributable => churn_90d = 0 =>
  freq_mult = 0.5 (the minimum). Best-effort; never fails the review.
- REVERSIBILITY FLOOR (det/hybrid), GATED: if the change touches a hard-to-reverse surface (migration dir,
  exported public API, persisted/serialization format, deletion, released artifact) AND the finding
  identifies at least one true consequence (`impact_base > 0`), then `reversibility_floor = 0.6`, else 0.
  The `impact_base > 0` gate is deliberate: it lifts a GENUINE defect on a one-way-door surface to >=0.6 but
  does NOT manufacture impact for a clean/no-consequence finding on the same file (fixes the "fires
  unconditionally, over-inflates nits" hole).
- DETECTION AMPLIFIER: `amp = 1.0 if (silent_failure or escapes_automation) else 0.8`. Both are LLM-emitted
  booleans in `code_review_verification_model` (a code-review detection judgment), defaulting to false (=>
  amp 0.8) when absent.
- EXPLICIT COMPOSE ORDER (single expression, no ambiguity):
  `impact_base = MAX(prod_lane, maint_lane); impact = max(min(1.0, impact_base * amp), reversibility_floor)`.
- RE-ANCHOR: drop the 0.33 attribute floor; co-calibrate tier values + block threshold on the labeled set
  (below) so a maintainability landmine reaches ~0.8+ and a nit ~0.15-0.2.
- CARVED SUB-QUESTION SET (audit-approved) with EXPLICIT lane + tier (ADD to, do NOT replace, the existing
  severity_attributes — CodeSeverityAttrs extends the base five):
    prod lane:  data_loss_without_recovery=0.9, security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere=0.9,
                silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision=0.9, capability_degraded=0.6
    maint lane: unversioned_published_contract_break=0.9, safety_net_removal_without_replacement=0.9,
                contract_drift=0.6, hidden_invariant=0.6, implicit_coupling=0.3, dead_code=0.3
  Each serious binary carries its charitable carve-out in its verify-prompt definition.

## Implementation mechanics + calibration data
- SUB-ANSWER SCHEMA: emit the tier-tagged binaries (bool) + `likelihood` (enum) + `silent_failure` /
  `escapes_automation` (bool) as NEW fields on a `code_review_verification_model()` whose severity_attributes
  is `CodeSeverityAttrs` EXTENDING the shared 5 attrs — a SINGLE aggregate Pass-2 verify call, no added LLM
  calls. Registered as a `code_review_verification` contract; code-review's verify step points at it (it
  reuses the kernel `verification` contract today). Each new binary defaults false / likelihood 'common' /
  detection false, so an older/absent verifier ABSTAINS (no true binary => lane 0 => impact 0).
- DET ENRICHMENT: `code_review_decide` computes `churn_90d` (git) + the reversibility surface (changed-file
  paths) per finding and merges them into that finding's severity_attributes BEFORE `pass3_over_findings`,
  so `impact_code(attrs)` reads both the LLM binaries and the DET signals from one dict. Best-effort.
- PER-GATE DISPATCH: `code_review_decide` passes `impact_fn=impact_code`. Plan-review is byte-unchanged
  (it threads impact_plan; nothing here touches it) — asserted by test.
- CALIBRATION/LABELED SET: a curated fixture `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` of
  code-review findings each tagged HIGH (block-worthy) or NIT, provenance = the dogfooded code-review corpus +
  this program's held-out adjudication (initial ~31 labeled findings; grows via the data-capture child).
- ADR: this novel two-lane model is an architectural decision — record it under `docs/adr/`.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` (`impact_code`), `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py`
  (`code_review_verification_model` + CodeSeverityAttrs), `src/rebar/llm/code_review/` (verify-step contract
  wiring, the DET enrichment in the decide op, the verify prompt), the labeled fixture + re-anchoring
  calibration test, and the ADR. Plan-review impact unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `impact_code = max(min(1.0, MAX(prod_lane, maint_lane) * amp), reversibility_floor)` per the explicit
      compose above, with tier-tagged-binary MAX within each lane using the stated tier values and the stated
      likelihood_mult / freq_mult / reversibility_floor (impact_base>0 gated) / amp formulas. Unit-tested for
      each multiplier + the gate. Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_impact_code.py -v`.
- [ ] Carved serious/moderate/minor sub-question set implemented with charitable carve-outs, ADDED to (not
      replacing) severity_attributes via CodeSeverityAttrs; emitted in ONE aggregate Pass-2 call (no added
      LLM calls); reversibility/reach/change-frequency are DET/hybrid (diff + git) with a churn=0/freq=0.5
      fallback when git is unavailable.
- [ ] Per-gate dispatch via `impact_fn=impact_code`; a test asserts plan-review's decide path is unchanged
      (impact_plan still dispatched; impact_code never touches it); an ADR under docs/adr/ records the model.
- [ ] `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` exists (each row HIGH/NIT, provenance in a header
      comment or sibling README). A test asserts impact_code SEPARATES the classes on the fixture with a
      CONCRETE margin (NO "or explained" escape): `median(impact_code[HIGH]) - median(impact_code[NIT]) >=
      0.30`, AND that this median gap is strictly GREATER than the old mean `impact`'s gap on the same
      fixture (the redesign beats the old scalar). Validated diff-grounded before any threshold-down tuning
      (the tuning itself is the calibration child raptorial-galloping-dragon).
